question: Which mapping from a deprecated `camunda.client.zeebe.*` property to its documented replacement is NOT correct per the Camunda 8.8 reference?

a.text: The deprecation table maps `zeebe.audience` to `camunda.client.auth.audience`, so this mapping is correctly stated.

b.text: The table maps `zeebe.execution-threads` to `camunda.client.execution-threads`, so this mapping is correct.

c.text: The table maps `zeebe.prefer-rest-over-grpc` to `camunda.client.prefer-rest-over-grpc`, so this is correct as well.

d.text: This is the incorrect mapping. The documented replacement for `zeebe.scope` is `camunda.client.auth.scope` (in the auth namespace), not `camunda.client.scope`. Like `audience`, OAuth scope moves under `auth`.

explanation: OAuth properties from the deprecated `camunda.client.zeebe` namespace (such as `audience` and `scope`) move into `camunda.client.auth`, not directly under `camunda.client`. Non-auth settings (timeouts, addresses, threading, message size) move to the top-level `camunda.client.*` namespace.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot Starter - properties reference
